last_excerpt: already felt like homework. Georgia is warmer. Palatino is warmer still. The difference is a few pixels on the curve of a lowercase a. The lowercase a has two versions. The one in handwriting — single story, just a loop and a tail. The one in most printed fonts — double story, with the little roof on top. Kids learn one and then encounter the other and have to reconcile them. Same letter. Different object. g is the same way. Handwriting g and printed g are almost entirely unlike each other. The printed one has two loops stacked, the bottom one open. That open tail. Where it's going is unclear. The number 1 with serifs looks completely different from the number 1 without. With serifs it has a hat and a foot and it looks permanent. Without serifs it's just a line. Could be anything. A line could be anything.
last_concepts: the smell of chlorine on skin hours later
the gap between a mattress and a wall
the way a phone screen lights up a dark room
